subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the least squares solution to \(Ax=b\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Derive the normal equations for the least squares problem.

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Prove that the unique solution to the least squares problem is \((A^T A)^{-1}A^T b\).

subparts.label: (e)
subparts.text: Describe how to solve the least squares problem using the QR decomposition of \(A\).

problem.text: Define a normal matrix and prove that the following are equivalent.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\langle x,y\rangle_A=x^*Ay\) is an inner product on \(\mathbb{R}^m\) if and only if \(A\) is symmetric and positive definite.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Assume now that \(A\) is symmetric and positive definite. If \(x_*\) is the solution to \(Ax=b\) and \(\{p_1,\ldots,p_m\}\) is an orthonormal basis for \(\mathbb{R}^m\) with respect to \(\langle\ ,\ \rangle_A\) and \(x_*=\sum c_i p_i\), give a formula for the \(c_i\).

problem.text: If \(q_1,\ldots,q_n\) is an orthonormal basis for the subspace \(V \subset \mathbb{C}^m\) with \(m>n\), prove that the orthogonal projector onto \(V\) is \(QQ^*\), where \(Q\) is the matrix whose columns are the \(q_j\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that \(A\) has a Schur decomposition.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: If \(A\) has a collection of \(m\) linearly independent eigenvectors, show that \(A\) is diagonalizable.
